seoTitle: Mannering Park vs Manning Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mannering Park and Manning Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mannering Park vs Manning Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mannering Park and Manning Point.
heading: Mannering Park vs Manning Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mannering Park, New South Wales and Manning Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Manning Point has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Mannering Park (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mannering Park or Manning Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mannering Park has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Manning Point scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mannering Park scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Manning Point scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mannering Park or Manning Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Manning Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
